56.reason: [Error: The loop condition uses p1 instead of p, causing an infinite loop since p1 never changes; also p is initialized but not used correctly]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: The loop condition uses p1 instead of p, causing an infinite loop since p1 never changes; also p is initialized but not used correctly]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: The loop condition uses p1 instead of p, causing an infinite loop since p1 never changes. It should be while(p!=NULL) to iterate through the first polynomial.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  Loop condition should be p!=NULL; p1 is the dummy head and never changes, causing infinite loop and later NULL pointer dereference.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Should be while(p!=NULL) to iterate over actual nodes of first polynomial; current condition causes infinite loop and uses dummy head.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Logical error: loop condition checks p1 which is never updated inside the loop, causing an infinite loop. It should check p!=NULL.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Infinite loop: p1 never advances; should use 'p' which is p1->link and advance it]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [The loop condition should be 'p != NULL' instead of 'p1 != NULL'; p1 never changes in the loop, causing infinite loop or incorrect iteration]

96.reason: [Error: poly_muti returns a new list, but polynome_addition modifies the input lists and may cause memory issues; also the result of poly_muti is not freed]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Error: poly_muti returns a new list, but polynome_addition modifies the input lists and may cause memory issues; also the result of poly_muti is not freed]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: poly_muti returns a new list, but polynome_addition modifies the input lists and may cause memory issues or incorrect results. Also, the result of poly_muti is not freed, causing memory leak.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [poly_muti expects (Nodeptr, node*), but passes (Nodeptr, node*); however, poly_muti uses p1 as if it's a term, not a list head. Logic error: should multiply entire polynomial by one term of the other]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [poly_muti expects two polynomial heads, but passes Nodeptr1 (head) and p (a term node), causing misuse of data structure]

59.reason: Should use p->xishu instead of p1->xishu; p1 is the dummy head with uninitialized coefficient.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Error: Should use p->xishu and p->zhishu instead of p1->xishu and p1->zhishu, because p1 is the head node and p is the current term.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Should use p->xishu instead of p1->xishu; p1 is the dummy head with uninitialized coefficient.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Logical error: uses the head node p1 instead of the current term pointer p. Should be p->xishu.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Should use 'p->xishu' and 'p->zhishu' instead of 'p1->xishu' and 'p1->zhishu' because p1 is the head dummy node and doesn't hold term data]

84.reason: [Incorrect input termination: mixing scanf with getchar may leave newline in buffer causing premature exit or infinite loop]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.2 , reason:  This loop will not terminate correctly because scanf returns 0 on encountering newline, not EOF; causing undefined behavior.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Incorrect input termination: mixing scanf with getchar may leave newline in buffer causing premature exit or infinite loop]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [This loop condition may not correctly detect end-of-line; it uses EOF which may not occur on newline, leading to over-reading or hanging]
